channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: AI-Generated ‘Actor’ Tilly Norwood Drops a Music Video Ahead of the Oscars. It Sucks
item.description: More like Tilly Snore-wood.

item.title: Test du Redmagic 11 Air : le gaming sans la taxe « Pro »
item.description: Ce Redmagic 11 Air va droit au but : un écran, une autonomie record et un gros processeur de smartphone. Le tout pour 500 euros. Regardons ce qu'il a dans le ventre pour ce prix.

item.title: Amazon launches its healthcare AI assistant on its website and app
item.description: Health AI can answer questions, explain health records, manage prescription renewals, book appointments, and more.

item.title: DOGE employee stole Social Security data and put it on a thumb drive, report says
item.description: A whistleblower is accusing a former DOGE member of stealing a large number of Americans’ personal data while he was working at the Social Security Administration, with the plan of using it at his new job.

item.title: Pete Hegseth Is Pushing Defense Employees to Volunteer With DHS
item.description: The defense secretary has made clear that Pentagon managers are to encourage workers, including civilians, to volunteer to assist in the administration’s immigration crackdown.

item.title: Metadata company Gracenote is the latest to sue OpenAI for copyright infringement
item.description: AI companies have been spending a lot of time in court arguing copyright cases over the past year and the latest plaintiff is Gracenote, the metadata company owned by Nielsen. Axios reports that Gracenote is suing OpenAI for the unauthorized and unpaid use of both its metadata and its framework for connecting that information. Gracenote specializes in entertainment metadata, creating descriptions and identifiers for content that clients such as TV providers use to help their own customers with discovery. Most of the lawsuits against AI businesses have focused on the content used to train LLMs,...

item.title: Lego Gives in, Will Finally Make Mario Minifigures
item.description: The Lego and 'Super Mario Bros.' collaboration will bend to fan demand starting in 2027.

item.title: Test de la Zendure SolarFlow 2400 AC+ : avons-nous enfin trouvé la batterie solaire parfaite ?
item.description: Zendure range les câbles et passe au tout-en-un. Le SolarFlow 2400 AC+ n'est plus un simple hub, c'est un bloc massif de 2400 Wh qui s'installe en deux minutes sur une simple prise. On a pu le tester.

item.title: ‘Pokémon Pokopia’ is a game about rehabilitating a broken world — and I love it
item.description: The latest Pokémon game is a cozy life simulator like "Animal Crossing" or "Minecraft," but in a way that feels more grounded to our actual world.

item.title: Nvidia Hopes Multi-Frame Gen Will Finally Make Sense on Its GPUs
item.description: Nvidia promises you may finally get use out of that 240Hz refresh rate on your expensive QD-OLED monitor.

item.title: Why is Volkswagen suddenly planning one of the biggest job cuts in auto industry history?
item.description: Slowing demand, tariffs, and geopolitical uncertainty are cutting into the profits of Volkswagen’s most lucrative brands. Volkswagen plans to slash 50,000 jobs in Germany by 2030 after reporting a sharp drop in annual operating profit for 2025.

item.title: Razer’s BlackShark V2 Pro gaming headset is $90, which is a new low price
item.description: If you’re looking for a reliable wireless gaming headset under $100, the Xbox version of the Razer BlackShark V2 Pro is worth a look. Right now, it’s down to a new low of $89.99 ($110 off) at Amazon in black and white. Despite being designed for Xbox consoles, the esports-inspired headset can connect wirelessly to […]

item.title: Bait and Switch? RFK Jr.’s FDA Pivots on ‘Promising’ Autism Treatment Leucovorin
item.description: The FDA ultimately ruled against approving leucovorin for autism, citing a lack of "sufficient data."

item.title: 'The cloud threat landscape is rapidly shifting': Google research warns hackers are targeting third parties and software flaws to gain entry
item.description: New Google report suggests cloud misconfigurations are on the decline, forcing crooks to pivot.

item.title: This Custom Lego-Inspired Mac Mini Case Is Retrofuturism Done Right
item.description: It looks super cool and it houses the computer that the designer uses every day.

item.title: ‘The Matrix 5’ Is Still in the Works, Celebrate With This Incredible Neo Toy
item.description: Writer Drew Goddard gave io9 an update on the new film, which just so happened to be timed with a new Hot Toys Neo.

item.title: YouTube surpasses Disney, Paramount, WBD in 2025 ad revenue
item.description: YouTube pulled in $40.4 billion in ad revenue last year, which is more than Disney, NBC, Paramount, and Warner Bros. Discovery combined.

item.title: Pokopia Pokédex review: a classic, reimagined
item.description: Thirty years ago, Nintendo struck gold with the release of the original Pokémon games. Soon, kids across the world were introduced to dozens of magical creatures that they could befriend and learn more about using sophisticated electronic encyclopedias known as Pokédexes. The first Pokédexes were reflections of how the Pokémon world was shaped by advanced […]...

item.title: Epic is increasing the price of Fortnite's V-Bucks currency
item.description: The real world price of impulse-buying Fortnite skins is going up, Epic has announced. Not because skins themselves are getting more expensive on paper, but because V-Bucks, Fortnite's digital currency, is. The same prices you paid for bundles of V-Bucks in February will now effectively earn you fewer bucks starting on March 19, along with several other Fortnite-related pricing changes.Epic will still offer bundles of V-Bucks starting at $8.99 and running all the way to $89.99, but with a new "conversion rate.” The new bundle prices breakdown as follows:$8.99 will get you 800 V-Bucks, down fro...

item.title: Anker’s Discounted Power Station Can Power Your Next Camping Trip
item.description: Go off-grid camping for a long weekend with my favorite portable power station from Anker. It’s on sale.

item.title: YouTube Expands AI Deepfake Detection Tool to Politicians, Won’t Say If Trump Is Included
item.description: The tool lets verified users request unauthorized AI-generated videos featuring their likeness to be taken down.

item.title: Tilly Norwood's music video is so bad that I think we can all breathe a sigh of relief — for now
item.description: Particle6 has released AI actor Tilly Norwood's first music video, and it's awful for all the reasons you imagined.

item.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/Atlas Has Another Surprise: It’s Full of Alcohol
item.description: Analysis of the tail of 3I/Atlas reveals that it contains an anomalous proportion of methanol, a class of alcohol used in fuels.

item.title: AI-powered apps struggle with long-term retention, new report shows
item.description: AI can drive stronger early monetization for apps, but sustaining value remains the challenge, RevenueCat's latest report finds.

item.title: Amazon wins a temporary injunction against Perplexity's Comet browser
item.description: Amazon has secured a temporary win in its fight with Perplexity over the use of AI shopping bots. Bloomberg reported that a San Francisco federal court has determined that Perplexity must stop using its Comet web browser's AI agent to make purchases for users on Amazon's marketplace. The AI company will have a week to appeal the decision, otherwise it has been ordered to stop accessing any password-protected areas of Amazon's systems and destroy its copies of Amazon's data while the two companies continue to argue their cases. "Amazon has provided strong evidence that Perplexity, through its ...
